Comparison

Reputation.com vs Revix

Reputation.com is an enterprise reputation management platform built for large restaurant chains, franchises, and multi-brand operations. Revix is an AI review reply engine for independent restaurants that charges per response. If you're a single-location or small-group restaurant evaluating Reputation.com, this page explains what you'd actually pay, what you'd use, and where the money goes.

Reputation.com vs Revix: the short version

Revix

Revix is an AI-powered Google review reply engine. Claude Sonnet writes each response using your restaurant's context (menu, tone, past reviews). Pay per reply: 99 Ft (~$0.29) for positive, 199 Ft (~$0.49) for negative reviews. A typical restaurant pays 3,000-10,000 Ft/month ($8-28). No contract, no setup fee. Connect your Google Business Profile in 10 minutes. Built for independent restaurants, cafes, and hotels.

Reputation.com vs Revix: feature comparison

Reputation.comRevix
Price
Pricing transparency
Contract
Onboarding time
Review monitoring platforms
AI review replies
Listings management
Delivery app integration
Item-level sentiment analysis
Staff coaching from feedback
Multi-brand management
Natural language analytics
Competitor benchmarking
Fake review appeal drafts
Guest profiling
Setup fee
Target market

Reputation.com vs Revix: where each wins

Reputation.com wins: enterprise-scale listings management

Reputation.com publishes your menus, hours, photos, and business details across every directory and platform simultaneously. For a chain with 50+ locations where a menu change needs to propagate to Google, Yelp, Apple Maps, Facebook, and 40+ other directories, this saves hundreds of hours annually. Revix does not manage listings — it focuses on review intelligence and AI replies.

Reputation.com wins: delivery app integration and staff coaching

Reputation.com integrates with Uber Eats, DoorDash, Grubhub, and other delivery platforms to pull ratings and feedback into one dashboard. It also provides staff coaching dashboards — location managers can see which staff-related feedback appears in reviews and act on it. These are operational tools for chains managing hundreds of employees across locations. Revix does not offer delivery integration or staff coaching features.

Reputation.com wins: multi-brand franchise hierarchy

If you manage multiple restaurant brands under one parent company, Reputation.com's franchise hierarchy lets you view performance at the brand, region, and location level. Corporate can enforce brand-approved response templates while giving location managers limited editing ability. This governance model is essential for franchises. Revix works per Google Business Profile connection — no hierarchy or brand governance layer.

Revix wins: AI reply quality for independent restaurants

Reputation.com's response system prioritizes brand consistency with templates that work across hundreds of locations. The result is professional but formulaic. Revix uses Claude Sonnet 4.6 with your specific restaurant context: menu items, cuisine type, past review patterns, and your preferred tone. When a guest mentions "the truffle risotto was outstanding," Revix knows that dish and crafts a reply that references it. Template-based systems can't match that specificity.

Revix wins: per-reply pricing (20-70x cheaper for SMB)

A restaurant getting 30 Google reviews/month would pay Reputation.com an estimated $500-2,000/month. With Revix, responding to those 30 reviews costs roughly 5,000 Ft ($14) total. Even at the low end of Reputation.com's range, that's 35x more expensive. You only pay when Revix writes a reply — no flat fee during slow months, no annual commitment locking you in.

Revix wins: fake review defense

When a suspicious review appears, Revix generates an appeal draft citing specific Google review policies, with evidence points and suggested language for removal requests. Reputation.com monitors reviews but does not generate policy-based appeal drafts. For restaurants facing review bombing or competitor-planted fake reviews, having an AI-generated appeal ready in minutes saves hours of policy research.

Reputation.com vs Revix: FAQ

Does Reputation.com publish pricing?

No. Reputation.com does not list prices on its website. You need to request a demo and go through a sales conversation to receive a custom quote. Based on user reports across G2, Capterra, and industry forums, estimates range from $500 to $2,000+ per month depending on location count and feature tier. This lack of pricing transparency makes it difficult for small restaurant owners to evaluate the tool without a significant time investment in the sales process.

Can a single restaurant use Reputation.com?

Technically yes, but Reputation.com's platform, pricing, and sales process are designed for multi-location businesses and enterprise chains. A single restaurant would likely receive a quote of $500+/month for features built for franchise operations — multi-brand hierarchy, staff coaching dashboards, delivery app integration — that a single location doesn't need. Revix is designed for exactly this use case: a single restaurant paying $8-28/month for AI review replies with 10-minute setup.

Does Revix monitor reviews on platforms other than Google?

No. Revix monitors and responds to Google reviews only. For restaurants, Google typically accounts for 80-90% of review volume and has the highest impact on local search visibility. If you need Yelp, TripAdvisor, DoorDash, or Uber Eats review monitoring, Reputation.com or a dedicated multi-platform tool would be needed alongside Revix.

How does Revix AI reply quality compare to Reputation.com?

Reputation.com uses brand-approved response templates with AI assistance — designed for consistency across hundreds of locations. The replies are professional but formulaic. Revix uses Claude Sonnet 4.6 with RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) that pulls your restaurant's specific context: menu items, cuisine style, past reviews, and your preferred tone. For a review mentioning a specific dish, Revix references that dish by name and context. Template-based systems trade specificity for brand consistency.

What does Reputation.com's item-level sentiment analysis do?

Reputation.com can analyze review text to identify sentiment about specific menu items — for example, detecting that "tacos" are mentioned positively 85% of the time while "wait time" appears negatively 60% of the time. This helps chains identify which menu items perform well and which need attention across locations. Revix provides per-review sentiment analysis and topic detection, but Reputation.com's item-level aggregation across 50+ locations gives enterprise chains a menu optimization angle.

How long does Reputation.com take to set up?

Implementation typically takes weeks to months depending on location count and integration complexity. Enterprise accounts with POS, CRM, and delivery platform integrations can take 2-3 months. Revix connects to your Google Business Profile in 10 minutes — no integrations, no account manager meetings, no data migration. The first AI reply can be generated the same day.

Can Revix handle 50+ locations?

Revix works per Google Business Profile connection. Technically, you can connect 50+ locations. But Revix is optimized for 1-20 restaurant locations. At 50+ locations with multi-platform needs, listings management, delivery integration, and franchise governance, a platform like Reputation.com makes more sense for centralized management and operational reporting.

AI review replies without the enterprise price tag

Claude Sonnet quality replies tuned to your restaurant. Per-response pricing. No contract. Live in 10 minutes.
